justification: Emory news article explicitly names both the Responsible AI Initiative and the Emory AI Group as the lead of the enterprise-wide effort.
HEC Montréal news page documents the renewal of affiliation/partnership between HEC Montréal and Université de Montréal, naming both parties.
Same HEC Montréal news page documents the tripartite renewal including Polytechnique Montréal and Université de Montréal.
Mila's own About page states it is 'the result of a unique collaboration between Université de Montréal and McGill University, in close collaboration with Polytechnique Montréal and HEC Montréal'.
Mila's About page explicitly names McGill University as a founding partner university of the institute.
Mila's About page explicitly names Polytechnique Montréal as a close collaboration partner in the institute's founding.
Mila's About page explicitly names HEC Montréal as a close collaboration partner in the institute's founding.
Mila's About page states that Mila and its sister institutes Amii (Alberta) and Vector (Ontario) play a central role in the Pan-Canadian Artificial Intelligence Strategy.
IBM Canada press release announcing the December 2020 collaboration between IBM and Mila to advance AI and machine learning research using open-source technology.
Amnesty International press release on the December 2018 joint study with Element AI measuring online abuse against women on Twitter.
SMU Newsroom article announcing Element AI's strategic partnership with Singapore Management University, naming both parties explicitly.
Reports on IASEAI's inaugural conference held at OECD Headquarters in Paris, documenting the partnership between IASEAI and OECD as host.
Lawyers' Committee statement on the AI Civil Rights Act introduction names Sen. Markey and the organization's partnership in introducing the legislation.
Official California Governor press release naming Newsom and IBM (along with Google, Adobe, Microsoft) in a signed MOU for AI workforce training.
Official NY Governor press release announcing Hochul's Empire AI Consortium with the $275 million investment housed at University at Buffalo.
